rag_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure that all identifiers used in assumptions and guarantees are visible in the current AADL scope and properly declared or referenced.
Place component type contracts, including input assumptions and output guarantees, within the appropriate component type annexes to maintain structural correctness.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or helper declarations within the same AGREE annex to avoid unresolved references.
Ensure type consistency between declared helpers and their usage in AGREE expressions to prevent type mismatch errors.
Validate that all referenced identifiers and types exist in the AADL model before including them in AGREE contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and placed in valid component type or implementation scopes.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference concrete bounds or named constants when defining input envelopes or output ranges.
anti_patterns: Avoid referencing identifiers or types that are not declared or visible in the current AADL scope, as this leads to validation errors.
Do not mix implementation-level bindings with component type contracts unless explicitly allowed by the AADL model structure.
irrelevant_topics: Implementation-level bindings in component type scopes
Subcomponent port usage in component type contracts
